programme.desc: An enigmatic killer shoots dead a businessman spending Thanksgiving at a hotel in the city, then takes his glasses, jewellery and ID to Washington Dulles airport, which has been brought to a stand-still by a massive snowstorm. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherley(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee deals with his father's recent death by writing him a letter outlining the details of his latest investigation. The case involved tracking down a hactivist collective known as Krampus, who were thought to have been responsible for an incident in which the internet crashed all over Washington, DC. McGee and Abby brought in three known cyber-terrorists for questioning, and discover evidence that links the trio to an attempted breach of the Congress e-mail server and archive. However, when this line of inquiry reaches a dead-end, the team picks up a new lead that suggests an isolated hacker from Wisconsin may be responsible. This 'lone wolf' scenario does not seem plausible to McGee, though, as he recalls a lesson his late father taught him. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Confidential files are leaked by an anonymous hacker, who threatens to release more unless the Navy agrees to renew a contract with a struggling shipbuilding firm. The Navy Secretary suspects the company's owner Philip Wickes is to blame, and asks NCIS to join forces with the PsyOps unit in an elaborate ruse to bring the culprit out of hiding. Meanwhile, Gibbs' colleagues begin to notice his attraction to his opposite number Samantha Ryan. Guest starring Jamie Lee Curtis(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee deals with his father's recent death by writing him a letter outlining the details of his latest investigation. The case involved tracking down a hactivist collective known as Krampus, who were thought to have been responsible for an incident in which the internet crashed all over Washington, DC. McGee and Abby brought in three known cyber-terrorists for questioning, and discover evidence that links the trio to an attempted breach of the Congress e-mail server and archive. However, when this line of inquiry reaches a dead-end, the team picks up a new lead that suggests an isolated hacker from Wisconsin may be responsible. This 'lone wolf' scenario does not seem plausible to McGee, though, as he recalls a lesson his late father taught him. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Zubin arranges for Ric to be reunited with his estranged son, a situation that becomes complicated when it transpires his girlfriend is six months pregnant and hooked on drugs. When she goes into premature labour, it falls to Jess and Zubin to save the baby. Connie leaves Will to handle an emergency heart operation on his own, but Mickie comes to the rescue, displaying surprising surgical skills. Former EastEnders actress Lucy Speed guest stars as a patient expecting quads(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Terry tracks down the paedophile who abused him as a child and, enraged by the old man's lack of remorse, resorts to physical violence. Yvonne tries to stop pregnant teenager Shirley selling her baby, but her well-meaning intervention has unexpected and potentially tragic consequences. Debbie learns her nanny has been hiding a shocking secret and Phil consoles Samantha, who is now convinced her missing daughter Abi has been abducted(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Ramani fears Terry has taken the law into his own hands when both he and Malcolm Willard go missing, but it seems the aged paedophile has more to fear from Terry's brother Ben, who is also looking for revenge. programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Poplar is gripped by an outbreak of dysentery, but that's the least of mum-to-be Marie Amos's worries when her husband Tony is arrested for gross indecency in the gentlemen's toilets and faces a criminal hearing. Nurse Crane meets Dolores McEvoy, a pregnant Irish woman who is clearly malnourished - but when she visits the woman's home, it turns out she has given a false address and has disappeared. Trixie is delighted when Tom asks her to oversee her first official church duty since their engagement, while at Nonnatus House, Fred tackles a rat infestation - only for Sister Monica Joan to work against him. With guest appearances by Cara Theobold (Downton Abbey) and Richard Fleeshman (Coronation Street)(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Winifred's eyes are opened to Poplar's seedy underworld when she cares for expectant prostitute Bridget Cole. Horrified by the young woman's lack of awareness, she draws on her teaching experience, and with help from Sgt Noakes sets out to promote safe sex to all local working girls. Barbara struggles to understand why expectant father Frank Robbins would pressure his wife to bear him a son, while Sister Monica Joan is feeling sidelined - until she finds herself unexpectedly assisting Shelagh with an emergency birth. Trixie invites herself along to a meeting between Tom and the Bishop - but things get tense when the idea of the couple moving to Newcastle is raised(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A Christian Scientist couple refuse medicine for their newborn baby, leaving Sister Julienne questioning the nature of absolute faith. However, the case becomes more complicated when the parents are accused of harming their child. Barbara uses her initiative to communicate with a pregnant Sylheti woman who cannot speak English, although using the patient's young son to translate provides new problems. Sister Mary Cynthia returns to Nonnatus House and a woman catches handyman Fred's eye(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A diabetic teenager faces a tough time after falling pregnant, a situation complicated by her boyfriend's criminal past. Nurse Crane offers what support she can - but the girl's life is endangered when the young lovers go on the run. A case of petty theft brings a group of Irish travellers to the attention of the midwives, who end up treating a newborn infant and looking after an expectant mother, and it seems Fred's romance is on the up when he asks Violet to accompany him to a fundraising dance(n)

programme.title: Father Brown

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Dwayne attends the funeral of local barber Cedrik Verga and shortly after is puzzled to receive a message from beyond the grave - a text from the deceased claiming 'I was murdered'. Given that his phone was in the coffin, is this a sick prank or something altogether more sinister? As the inquest delivers a lawful killings verdict, IPCC investigator Vicky Sharpe calls on the Lyell team to investigate the connection between the events and review the police evidence, but Sharpe's relationship with the family of the teenager and her suspicion of the firearms division lead Nikki and Thomas to distrust her motives. programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Poplar readies itself for Christmas 1960 with a festive bus trip to see the Regent Street lights, while a visit from a BBC film crew promises to make it a festive holiday to remember. At Nonnatus, things are tense as Sister Monica Joan determines to bring the Christmas spirit early - despite Sister Evangelina's disgust. programme.title: Gwesty Aduniad
programme.desc: Family and friend reunions set in a rural Wales hotel where every guest has one objective - to meet someone from their past. First up is Neil Pearson from Rhyl who was adopted as a baby and now wants to know more about his birth mother. Will the hotel have any answers?(n)

programme.title: Hewlfa Drysor
programme.desc: Game show in which three teams drive round different areas of Wales in search of treasure, combining their general knowledge skills, understanding of history and map-reading skills as they race to get their first(n)

programme.title: Cofio 'Dolig Teulu Ni
programme.desc: Over the past century the way Christmas has been celebrated in Wales has been completely transformed. This programme offers an opportunity for families to re-visit one Christmas Day in the history of their family, and behind the tinsel and turkey, reveals the story and situation of their ancestors at the time. Here, one family goes back to 1961 and the other goes back to 1984(n)

programme.title: Hansh
programme.desc: Tunes, comedy and fresh faces(n)

programme.title: C'mon Midffild
programme.desc: Arthur Picton loses his cool on the football pitch and lashes out at the referee, who is also a man of the cloth(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: CBeebies Hansel & Gretel
programme.desc: The stars of CBeebies bring the story of Hansel and Gretel to life, with a vibrant 1980s twist, and a number of classic pop hits from the decade. Featuring Cat Sandion and Molly and Mack's Joshua Haynes as the intrepid youngsters, alongside Andy Day, Maddie Moate and Waffle the Wonder Dog, making his CBeebies panto debut(n)

programme.title: The Gruffalo
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of Julia Donaldson's best-selling children's book. It tells the tale of a little mouse who takes a walk through the woods in search of a nut, only to encounter a fox, an owl and a snake who all want to eat him. To escape their clutches, the quick-witted rodent creates an imaginary monster to scare away his attackers - but it turns out to be all too real. Robbie Coltrane heads the voice cast as the Gruffalo, with James Corden as the Mouse and Rob Brydon, John Hurt and Tom Wilkinson as the predators(n)

programme.title: The Gruffalo's Child
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of the sequel to Julia Donaldson's best-selling children's book, reuniting all the voice cast from the original. The Gruffalo has raised his daughter on tales of the big, bad mouse, the only creature in the forest he is frightened of. Despite her father's warnings, she sets off in search of this scary creature, along the way encountering the snake, owl and the fox. But when she still has no luck finding the evil rodent, she begins to think he does not exist after all. Robbie Coltrane, James Corden and Rob Brydon lend their voices, with Shirley Henderson as Gruffalo Jr(n)

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda 3 (2016)
programme.desc: The clumsy hero is reunited with his long-lost father, whose help he will need when a supernatural foe threatens all of China. Father and son travel to a secret village populated by other pandas, and must train them in martial arts so they can defend their home. Animated comedy sequel, with the voices of Jack Black, Bryan Cranston, Angelina Jolie and Dustin Hoffman(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble spend Christmas with the animals. Two Siberian tiger cubs take their first steps into the park - and have their first encounter with cars. Elsewhere, the staff at the house face their biggest challenge of the year - finding a tree on the estate that is just the right size for the stately home, chop it down, and get it inside without causing any damage. Plus, Jean Johansson mucks in with the keepers to hand out Christmas treats to their much-loved creatures(n)

programme.title: Toy Story 3 (2010)
programme.desc: Woody, Buzz and the other toys accidentally end up in a daycare centre when their owner grows up. At first this seems like the perfect new home for them, but when they realise the dangers lurking under the surface, they begin to formulate an escape plan. Pixar animated adventure sequel, with the voices of Tom Hanks, Tim Allen, Joan Cusack, Michael Keaton and Ned Beatty(n)

programme.title: Frozen (2013)
programme.desc: A princess with the power to freeze anything she touches loses control of her abilities and plunges the kingdom into a perpetual winter. She goes into hiding, but her sister remains determined that she can be saved from her own magic and sets off on a quest to find her, aided by a mountain man and a living snowman. Disney animated fantasy adventure, with the voices of Kristen Bell and Idina Menzel(n)

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: The Wrong Trousers
programme.desc: Nick Park's Oscar-winning animation featuring the bumbling inventor and his faithful dog, whose lives are turned upside down after foolishly offering board and lodgings to villainous penguin Feathers McGraw. With the voice of Peter Sallis(n)

programme.title: Beauty and the Beast (2017)
programme.desc: A prince is transformed into a hideous monster as punishment for his selfishness, and can only regain his human form by finding true love. Salvation comes in the form of a spirited young inventor, who offers to take her father's place when he is imprisoned in the monster's castle. But as romance blossoms between the unlikely pair, a jealous rival suitor only sees a beast to be slain. Live action remake of the Disney animated fairytale, starring Emma Watson, Dan Stevens and Luke Evans, with Ewan McGregor, Emma Thompson and Ian McKellen playing the Beast's entourage of living ornaments(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The Beales and Mitchells gather to celebrate Keanu and Louise's return to the Square - but unbeknown to everyone, all hell is about to break loose. A mix-up over what to wear on a night out embarrasses Linda and she ends up drowning her sorrows in the middle of the Square - unaware her Christmas surprise has arrived in the shape of prodigal son Lee. Martin is filled with panic after the previous night's incident, leaving Bex and Sonia concerned for his welfare, and Denise is determined to prove Sheree is not all she says she is(n)

programme.title: Take Off with Bradley and Holly
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh and Holly Willoughby present a special Christmas family entertainment show, which offers members of the audience the chance to win the holiday of a lifetime to Lapland. The show will also feature special games, surprises and appearances by celebrity guests(n)

programme.title: A Christmas Carol
programme.desc: Scrooge is confronted with another spirit, which has assumed a familiar form drawn from his own tortured past. He witnesses the present day, and the seemingly happy Christmas enjoyed by the Cratchit family - but also learns there are cracks beneath the surface which he is responsible for making. The final ghost arrives to give Scrooge a glimpse of what the future holds - and a tragedy that will force him to confront the consequences of his misdeeds(n)

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee and Lucy pop out on a last-minute Christmas Eve mission, leaving their youngsters in the apparently capable hands of Geoffrey - cutting more of a strict and stuffy Victorian gent than usual - and wife Wendy. Alas, things don't quite go to plan for the outward-bound duo, as a theft and unexpected trip into deep countryside await. Not only that, but there are encounters with an aggressive bull in a field and a terrifying truck driver to endure. Meanwhile, back at home, Lee's lovably feckless father Frank turns up to offer gifts - in his own inimitably festive way. Lee Mack, Sally Bretton and Bobby Ball star(n)

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Alice suggests an unconventional nativity play starring herself and Hugo as Mary and Joseph, and adds an extra touch of authenticity by going into labour mid-performance. The coincidence is so striking that she asks Geraldine whether she has given birth to the Son of God - even though her baby is a girl. Festive episode from 1999, starring Dawn French and Emma Chambers(n)

programme.title: Midnight Mass - BBC
programme.desc: Live Christmas Eve service from Croydon Minster, led by Revd Dr Andrew Bishop. The preacher is the Bishop of Croydon, the Rt Revd Jonathan Clark. The choir, directed by Ronny Krippner, sing Mozart's Great Credo Mass in C and lead the congregation in a number of well-loved carols, including It Came Upon a Midnight Clear and Hark! The Herald Angels Sing, accompanied by organist Tom Little(n)

programme.title: Made in Dagenham (2010)
programme.desc: The female employees making upholstery at Ford's London plant in the 1960s campaign for the right to equal pay, only for the company to declare their work is unskilled. The women embark on a national protest, taking their grievance all the way to the government and demanding a change in the law. Fact-based comedy drama, starring Sally Hawkins, Miranda Richardson and Bob Hoskins(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty presents a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Stick Man
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of the children's book by author Julia Donaldson and illustrator Axel Scheffler, the creators of modern classic The Gruffalo. It follows the title character on an incredible adventure across the seasons as he runs from a playful dog, gets thrown into a river, escapes from a swan's nest and even ends up on top of a fire. Will he ever get back to his family in time for Christmas? Martin Freeman heads the voice cast as Stick Man, with Hugh Bonneville, Russell Tovey, Rob Brydon and Sally Hawkins, plus Jennifer Saunders as the narrator(n)

programme.title: Zog
programme.desc: Keen but accident-prone dragon Zog gets himself into mischief while learning how to fly, roar, breathe fire and fight knights in his first four years at Dragon School. Each year he meets a kindly young girl who patches up his bumps and bruises, but can she help him with his trickiest challenge yet? It's year five and he has to capture a princess. Animated adaptation of Julia Donaldson and Axel Scheffler's book, featuring the voices of Lenny Henry, Tracey Ullman, Hugh Skinner, Patsy Ferran and Kit Harington(n)

programme.title: Piper (2016)
programme.desc: Animated Pixar short, directed by Alan Barillaro. A mother bird tries to teach her little one how to find food for herself, but encounters a traumatic experience(n)

programme.title: Christmas Day Mass
programme.desc: St Joseph's Catholic Church in Bradford is the venue for this festive act of worship, with Bradford Catholic Youth Choir singing Mozart's Missa Brevis in C and leading the congregation in well-loved carols including O Come All Ye Faithful, Candlelight Carol and O Little Town of Bethlehem. The service is led by the Revd Christopher Angel and the Celebrant is the Bishop of Leeds, the Rt Revd Marcus Stock. The choir is directed by Thomas Leech and Alexander Kyle, and the organist is David Pipe(n)

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Close Shave
programme.desc: Nick Park's Oscar-winning animated characters embark on their third adventure. Wallace falls in love with the proprietor of the local wool shop - only for Gromit to be framed for sheep rustling. With the voices of Peter Sallis and Anne Reid(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Fearne Cotton and Clara Amfo present a festive edition of the programme celebrating the biggest hits and most successful acts of the year, concluding with the all-important Christmas Number One. The programme also features appearances by some of the hottest names in pop music, both in the UK and worldwide, including Lewis Capaldi, Labrinth and AJ Tracey(n)

programme.title: Moana (2016)
programme.desc: To save her island home from a terrible curse, a brave Polynesian teenager must set sail and journey into the great blue yonder with the aid of a legendary hero. Disney animation, with the voices of Dwayne Johnson, Auli'i Cravalho, Rachel House and Temuera Morrison(n)

programme.title: The Snail and the Whale
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of Julia Donaldson and Axel Scheffler's children's book. It tells the tale of a tiny snail who longs to see the world and hitches a ride on the tail of a huge humpback whale. Sally Hawkins and Rob Brydon head the voice cast as the title characters, with Cariad Lloyd and Diana Rigg(n)

programme.title: The Queen
programme.desc: Her Majesty Queen Elizabeth II delivers her annual address in the Christmas Message to the Nation and Commonwealth(n)

programme.title: Finding Dory (2016)
programme.desc: Young clownfish Nemo and his dad Marlin set out on an underwater adventure to help their forgetful friend Dory find her parents, meeting the grouchy but well-meaning octopus Hank along the way. Pixar animated sequel, featuring the voices of Ellen DeGeneres, Albert Brooks and Ed O'Neill(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present the traditional festive edition for Christmas Day, which sees the return of six old favourites from past series. Chizzy Akudolu, Gemma Atkinson, Richard Arnold, Debbie McGee, Joe Sugg and Mark Wright take to the dancefloor to perform special Christmas-inspired routines, with judges Shirley Ballas, Bruno Tonioli, Motsi Mabuse and Craig Revel Horwood giving their thoughts. However, it's down to the studio audience to decide which couple should lift the Silver Star trophy(n)

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: The comedy star brings the latest series of his entertainment showcase to a conclusion with his annual festive special from the London Palladium. Tonight, global superstar Sharon Osbourne hands over her phone to play Send to All?, and Strictly Come Dancing judge Craig Revel Horwood is woken up by Michael in the middle of the night for the Midnight Gameshow. Plus, another Unexpected Star puts on a spectacular festive performance(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Mother Mildred declares that God is calling her to set up a branch house in the Outer Hebrides, so she is joined by several other members of the team, including Dr Turner, Shelagh, Mother Mildred, Trixie, Sister Julienne, Valerie, Lucille and Fred, many of whom have been struck down by flu and like the idea of convalescing in the fresh Scottish air. However, as they set about establishing an antenatal clinic and treating the local population, they must battle not only the weather and challenging terrain, but the suspicions of the islanders. Back in Poplar, Reggie is inspired to attempt a world record(n)

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: One-off festive edition of the comedy, which returns after a nine-year break. The Shipmans travel to Wales to spend Christmas with the Wests, but for Gavin and Stacey, parenthood continues to present them with several challenges, and their 'baby' is now nine. Smithy also continues to commit to his fatherly role, making the journey to Barry at weekends and every second Wednesday to see 12-year-old Neil the Baby. Nessa has adopted her own style of mothering, while Pam, Mick and Gwen have become the epitome of doting grandparents. On Christmas Eve, Dawn makes a shocking discovery about Pete that jeopardises their marriage - again - while a visit to the pub becomes an eye-opener in more ways than one. Starring Mathew Horne, Joanna Page, James Corden and Ruth Jones(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Christmas Day arrives in Albert Square but there is not much festive joy for Phil. Having finally learned Sharon and Keanu's secret, he puts his revenge plan into action - although it is surely only a matter of time before he unleashes his full fury. Linda hits rock bottom, with the rest of her family in turmoil when she is nowhere to be found. Sonia catches Martin trying to flee and berates him for his selfishness, while the Slaters learn there is nothing like good neighbours(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Christmas is supposed to be a time for family and friends, so when it seems that this year Mrs Brown cannot please anyone, she wonders if it would have been better had she never been born. Enter Clyde, a mysterious figure claiming to be an angel, who tells the doting mother he can show her what life would have been like without her - and before she knows it, she finds herself in a world she doesn't recognise, and that doesn't recognise her. Brendan O'Carroll stars in this spoof of classic Christmas movie It's a Wonderful Life, with former Corrie star Kevin Kennedy (Curly Watts)(n)

programme.title: The Two Ronnies Sketchbook
programme.desc: Another chance to see Ronnie Barker's final screen appearance, originally aired two months after his death in 2005, in which he and Ronnie Corbett choose their favourite sketches, songs and monologues from their festive shows, including the Milkman's Christmas Message, Christmas Day in the Yukon and an interpretation of Alice Through the Looking Glass. Katie Melua sings Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas, before the duo sign off for the very last time(n)

programme.title: On Christmas Night
programme.desc: British rapper Stormzy tells the story of the first Christmas from Luke's Gospel, when angels proclaimed the news of Jesus's birth, and boys' choir Libera perform the Basque carol Sing Lullaby(n)

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee and Lucy pop out on a last-minute Christmas Eve mission, leaving their youngsters in the apparently capable hands of Geoffrey - cutting more of a strict and stuffy Victorian gent than usual - and wife Wendy. Alas, things don't quite go to plan for the outward-bound duo, as a theft and unexpected trip into deep countryside await. Not only that, but there are encounters with an aggressive bull in a field and a terrifying truck driver to endure. Meanwhile, back at home, Lee's lovably feckless father Frank turns up to offer gifts - in his own inimitably festive way. Lee Mack, Sally Bretton and Bobby Ball star(n)

programme.title: My Old Lady (2014)
programme.desc: A penniless New Yorker hopes to turn his fortunes around by selling his dead father's flat in Paris, only to find an eccentric elderly Englishwoman is living there - and under French law, he cannot sell until after she dies. As he gets to know his unexpected tenant, he discovers a side to his father that he never know. Comedy drama based on director Israel Horovitz's stage play, starring Maggie Smith, Kevin Kline and Kristin Scott Thomas(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty presents a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: The Good Dinosaur (2015)
programme.desc: In a world where the asteroid impact that wiped out the dinosaurs never happened, humans have evolved as a primitive species while the giant lizards remain the dominant life form on Earth. A young apatosaurus becomes separated from his family, but finds a friend in a feral human boy as he makes the dangerous journey home. Pixar animated adventure, with the voices of Raymond Ochoa and Jack Bright(n)

programme.title: Wreck-It Ralph (2012)
programme.desc: A video game villain grows tired of always being the bad guy, and ventures into other games in search of the chance to be a hero, unwittingly causing chaos in the process. When a deadly threat to the entire arcade appears, it falls to him to set things right. Animated adventure, with the voices of John C Reilly, Sarah Silverman and Jane Lynch(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Snow
programme.desc: Undercover cameras reveal how living in the snow tests animals to their limit. In New Zealand, cameras follow a group of kea parrots, revealing how friendship is at the heart of the lives of these sociable and highly intelligent birds. In the North Pole, cameras get up close to play-fighting polar bears, while on the other side of the planet at the South Pole, footage reveals the parental devotion that emperor penguins have as they try to secure the survival of their offspring and face some of the harshest conditions on Earth. In Tasmania, the spy wallaby finds love - discovering along the way an interesting courtship technique among wombats(n)

programme.title: Room on the Broom
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of the children's book by author Julia Donaldson and illustrator Axel Scheffler, the creators of modern classic The Gruffalo. It tells the story of a kind witch who invites a surprising number of animals to ride on her broom - much to the frustration of her cat. But when a fearsome dragon appears, what will the gang do? Gillian Anderson heads the voice cast as the witch, with Rob Brydon, Martin Clunes, Sally Hawkins and David Walliams as the animals, Timothy Spall as the dragon and Simon Pegg as narrator(n)

programme.title: The Boss Baby (2017)
programme.desc: A youngster believes his baby brother is actually a power-hungry tycoon sent to destroy his perfect family life. However, the duo must put aside their differences and work together when they uncover a plot to sabotage the company their parents work for. Animated comedy, featuring the voices of Alec Baldwin, Steve Buscemi, Jimmy Kimmel and Lisa Kudrow(n)

programme.title: Mimi and the Mountain Dragon
programme.desc: Animated adaptation of Michael Morpurgo's children's book about a brave but shy girl from a village that lives in constant fear of a terrible mountain dragon. On a snowy winter's night, she finds a baby dragon asleep in her family's woodshed, and sets out on a terrible journey through a blizzard to return the infant to its dreaded mother. Featuring an introduction by the author and a score by Oscar-winning composer Rachel Portman, performed by the BBC Philharmonic(n)

programme.title: The Jungle Book (2016)
programme.desc: A boy is raised by animals in the jungles of India, but his carefree life is threatened by the return of a fierce tiger with a grudge against humans. The youngster faces a difficult choice between remaining with his friends or returning to his own people. Live action remake of Disney's animated adventure based on Rudyard Kipling's stories, starring Neel Sethi, with the voices of Bill Murray, Ben Kingsley and Idris Elba(n)

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Matter of Loaf and Death
programme.desc: The duo open their own bakery, Top Bun, and business is soon booming - not least because a 'cereal killer' is targeting all the rival bakers in town. Even better, Wallace has caught the eye of local beauty Piella Bakewell. But Gromit is more concerned that his master might become the killer's next victim. With the voices of Peter Sallis and Sally Lindsay(n)

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge
programme.desc: City children Susan and John are spending their summer at Scatterbrook Farm, where they are amazed to discover the scarecrow of Ten Acre Field can come to life. But it seems this extraordinary new friendship has thrown the rhythm of the natural world out of kilter and now they must work to put things right. Mackenzie Crook writes, directs and stars in the first of two new dramas based on the books by Barbara Euphan Todd - last adapted 40 years ago in the popular ITV series with Jon Pertwee. Co-starring Steve Pemberton, Rosie Cavaliero and Vicki Pepperdine(n)

programme.title: Paddington 2 (2017)
programme.desc: The lovable little bear has settled into his new life with the Brown family in London, becoming a much-loved member of the local community - until his search for a birthday present for his aunt leads to him being framed as a thief. While the Browns try to clear Paddington's name, he tries to make himself at home in prison, winning over the other convicts and getting involved in an escape attempt. Comedy adventure, starring Hugh Bonneville, Hugh Grant and Sally Hawkins, with the voices of Ben Whishaw and Imelda Staunton(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The events of Christmas Day weigh heavy in Walford, with more than one family left ripped apart. Sharon and Keanu's worlds have been shattered - but how far has Phil gone? On a happier note, Gray and Chantelle are excited about renewing their wedding vows but their plans are ruined when the venue cancels. Patrick is unhappy about Denise meddling in his personal life and takes an extreme course of action(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts a festive edition of the comedy panel show in which the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure victory. Joining team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack for the Christmas fun are Liz Bonnin, Stephen Merchant, Sharon Osbourne and Ranj Singh(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker introduces highlights of the Boxing Day Premier League fixtures, including Leicester City v Liverpool at the King Power Stadium and Tottenham Hotspur v Brighton & Hove Albion at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. The Foxes and Reds had both made excellent starts to the season, with Brendan Rodgers' side emerging as surprise contenders for the title - or at the very least a Champions League place. Plus, action from Aston Villa v Norwich City at Villa Park, Bournemouth v Arsenal at the Vitality Stadium, Chelsea v Southampton at Stamford Bridge, Crystal Palace v West Ham United at Selhurst Park, Everton v Burnley at Goodison Park, Manchester United v Newcastle United at Old Trafford, and Sheffield United v Watford at Bramall Lane(n)

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: The comedy star brings the latest series of his entertainment showcase to a conclusion with his annual festive special from the London Palladium. Tonight, global superstar Sharon Osbourne hands over her phone to play Send to All?, and Strictly Come Dancing judge Craig Revel Horwood is woken up by Michael in the middle of the night for the Midnight Gameshow. Plus, another Unexpected Star puts on a spectacular festive performance(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The first three chefs are challenged to recreate Marcus' crab omelette Arnold Bennett, before the second trio take on Monica's puff pastry and vanilla mousseline layered dessert. Moving into the second round, the six chefs are then tasked to impress the judges with a signature dish of their own design(n)

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Rome
programme.desc: Celebrities of differing faiths and beliefs don walking boots to tackle the 1,000km Italian section of the ancient Via Francigena, which starts in Canterbury and finishes in Rome, where a private audience with the Pope awaits. In the first episode, actors Les Dennis and Lesley Joseph, dancer Brendan Cole, comedians Stephen K Amos and Katy Brand, athlete Greg Rutherford, singer Dana and TV host Mehreen Baig each arrive in Switzerland to find out who is sharing their trek. In just 15 days, they will walk some sections and travel some by minibus, though the final 100km will all be on foot. Narrated by Lee Ingleby(n)

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Rome
programme.desc: Pilgrims Les Dennis, Lesley Joseph, Brendan Cole, Stephen K Amos, Katy Brand, Greg Rutherford, Dana and Mehreen Baig visit Bagno Vignoni - well known for its hot springs - where Greg and Brendan take the plunge in the public pool. Meanwhile, at the top of the hillside, Dana, Stephen, Les and Katy visit the Roman bath formerly used by Popes, and explore the history of St Catherine of Siena, who also used to stay here. The group members travel on to the medieval city of Viterbo, from where they will have to walk every step of the final 100km to Rome. Narrated by Lee Ingleby(n)

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Rome
programme.desc: The Pilgrims arrive in Sutri, a medieval town with an even older history, where they visit a magnificent Roman amphitheatre, where Lesley Joseph auditions for 'producer'. Stephen K Amos, Mehreen Baig and Les Dennis visit a church, which was originally Etruscan and pagan before becoming Roman, then Christian and finally Catholic. Later, with Rome on the horizon, they get their first view of the Eternal City from Monte Mario park, heading to St Peter's Square. Then emotions run high as they learn Pope Francis has agreed to meet them in a private audience. Narrated by Lee Ingleby(n)

programme.title: Christmas at St Paul's
programme.desc: Documentary following preparations for the festive season at the famous London cathedral, featuring unprecedented behind-the-scenes access to the Christmas countdown. With more than 45,000 visitors attending 17 services and events, the pressure is on for the team gearing up for the busiest time of the year. Celebrities including Michael Palin perform at the most glamorous event in the cathedral's calendar, while three huge Christmas trees - donated by the Queen and weighing more than a ton - are hauled into place. There's also a look at the huge logistical challenge that goes into supplying enough consecrated wine and wafers for the thousands of people who attend Midnight Mass(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A retrospective look at TV appearances made over the years by award-winning actor Albert Finney, with interviews from the archive and classic clips capturing the milestones and highlights of his life and career. Narrated by Sylvia Syms(n)

programme.title: Murder on the Orient Express (1974)
programme.desc: An American businessman travelling on the Orient Express from Istanbul to Paris tries to hire Hercule Poirot to discover the source of the death threats that have been made against him. The sleuth refuses, but wakes up the following morning to learn the man has been stabbed to death while the train was held up by heavy snow - and there is no shortage of suspects among the passengers. Agatha Christie mystery, starring Albert Finney, Lauren Bacall, Ingrid Bergman, Sean Connery and Vanessa Redgrave(n)

programme.title: Death on the Nile (1978)
programme.desc: Detective Hercule Poirot investigates a murder on board a paddle steamer in Egypt. The victim was an unpopular heiress, whose outspoken manner put her at odds with most of her fellow passengers during their cruise on the Nile. Agatha Christie mystery, starring Peter Ustinov, Bette Davis, Maggie Smith, George Kennedy, Mia Farrow, Jane Birkin, Olivia Hussey, Angela Lansbury and David Niven(n)

programme.title: A Merry Tudor Christmas with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian recreates festive traditions from the reign of Henry VIII. With the aid of food historian Annie Gray, she prepares two royal feasts, including a stuffed boar's head served by a choir, and the ancestors of the modern mince pies and Christmas cake. Lucy also gets a taste of the rabble-rousing anarchy of the Lord of Misrule, listens to carol singers performing a song written by Henry VIII himself, and watches a special royal performance involving dancing stags(n)

programme.title: Carols from King's
programme.desc: The traditional celebration of Christmas from the candlelit Chapel of King's College, with the world-famous choir directed for the first time by former King's College Organ Scholar, Daniel Hyde, and a service led by the Dean, the Revd Dr Stephen Cherry, and the Chaplain, the Revd Andrew Hammond. A selection of old and new carols include the Sussex Carol (arrangement by Vaughan Williams), There Is No Rose (Elizabeth Maconchy) and O Holy Night (Adolphe Adam, arrangement by JE West). The Christmas story is told in the words of the King James Bible, with poems by Patrick Carey and Ruth Padel(n)

programme.title: The Morecambe and Wise Show: The Lost Tapes
programme.desc: The first of two episodes that have not been seen since their original broadcast in 1968, and were thought to be lost until they were found by an archive preservation expert in an abandoned cinema in Sierra Leone, West Africa. The episodes have also been transformed from black-and-white into colour, by decoding colour information held within the copies. In this episode, Chris Langford sings an old favourite, Old Devil Moon, and Michael Aspel guest stars(n)

programme.title: The Morecambe and Wise Show: The Lost Tapes
programme.desc: The second of two episodes of the comedy that were believed to be lost until recently, when they were uncovered inside a derelict cinema in Sierra Leone, West Africa by Philip Morris, an archive preservation expert who specialises in recovering missing tapes. Sketches in this episode, Eric and Ernie are joined by Ronnie Carroll for two Irish sketches, and Kenny Ball plays Samantha from High Society(n)

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman hosts the second round of the festive quiz featuring teams of famous graduates. This episode pits a team representing Birmingham City University, featuring The Archers' Carole Boyd and gardening expert Bunny Guinness, against alumni of Wadham College, Oxford, including journalists and broadcasters Anne McElvoy and Jonathan Freedland(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon discovers what happened to some of the most memorable businesses and entrepreneurs who have exited the famous Den doors over the past 17 series. Angela takes Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and new Dragon Sara Davies on a trip down memory lane when she shows them footage of themselves from former series, and she meets the latest, and youngest Dragon, Sara Davies, who has been a die-hard fan of the show since her university days(n)

programme.title: Gareth Malone's Christmas Concert
programme.desc: The choirmaster puts on a show for the hard-pressed staff at Watford General Hospital, who are among the 400,000 NHS employees who will be giving up their Christmas Day this year to care for patients. Responding to a message from the chief executive of the hard-working community hospital who wants to give her workers a much-needed festive boost, Gareth sets to work enlisting the help of staff, patients and family members to produce a spectacular concert full of wonder for everyone to enjoy(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig hosts a festive edition of the panel game, joined by regular panellist Alan Davies and guests Johnny Vegas, Sara Pascoe and Josh Widdicombe. Among the fascinating topics discussed are the aquatic equivalent of a sheepdog, why ice is slippery, and how big Dumbo's ears would have to be in order to fly. Plus, Sara reveals how her mother ruined the show for everyone and Alan achieves something he has wanted to do since QI first began(n)

programme.title: I'll Get This
programme.desc: Five celebrities go out for Christmas dinner together and between courses play a series of games, with the eventual loser having to pay for the meal. Taking part in this festive special are Strictly Come Dancing judge Shirley Ballas, comedian and TV presenter Joel Dommett, Love Island star Maura Higgins, MasterChef presenter Gregg Wallace and actor and funnyman Les Dennis(n)

programme.title: Great Expectations (2012)
programme.desc: A penniless orphan is chosen by a reclusive spinster to be a playmate for her cold-hearted ward, whom he falls in love with. As he grows up, a mysterious benefactor offers him the chance to rise into high society, but dark secrets lie behind both the object of his adoration and his newfound fortune. Period drama based on Charles Dickens' novel, starring Jeremy Irvine, Helena Bonham Carter, Ralph Fiennes and Holliday Grainger(n)

programme.title: Tamara Drewe (2010)
programme.desc: A woman returns to the sleepy village where she grew up, having transformed from an awkward teenager to a successful and confident journalist. She instantly becomes the object of obsession for every man in the neighbourhood, while her sex life becomes a source of fascination for two local girls. Comedy based on Posy Simmonds' comic strip, with Gemma Arterton, Dominic Cooper and Tamsin Greig(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Home for Christmas
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King create an Austrian linzertorte, roast goose and all the trimmings, and a hazelnut chocolate brownie with clementine sorbet, as they find out how guest Aled Jones spends his Christmas. Alongside the recipes and fun, there are also fantastic ideas for home-made edible Christmas presents(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Bea requires emergency surgery, so Kian takes matters into his own hands, despite having been suspended - will this be the end of his career? Essie has a run-in with new consultant Ben Sherwood, just as Sacha encourages her to give him a second chance - although he may soon regret it. Dominic throws himself into work after receiving his divorce papers for Christmas(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Chimpanzee (2012)
programme.desc: Wildlife documentary following the development of a young chimpanzee living in the forests of the Ivory Coast. The ape is left to fend for itself after its mother is killed in a confrontation with a rival troop, only to find a surprising ally in its own family group's alpha male. Narrated by Tim Allen(n)

programme.title: Rise of the Guardians (2012)
programme.desc: Magical beings from folklore, including Santa Claus, Jack Frost and the Easter Bunny, join forces to defeat the plans of an evil spirit, who intends to steal the dreams of children and use the power they grant him to dominate the world. Animated adventure, with the voices of Chris Pine, Alec Baldwin, Hugh Jackman, Isla Fisher and Jude Law(n)

programme.title: Carols from King's
programme.desc: The traditional celebration of Christmas from the candlelit Chapel of King's College, with the world-famous choir directed for the first time by former King's College Organ Scholar, Daniel Hyde, and a service led by the Dean, the Revd Dr Stephen Cherry, and the Chaplain, the Revd Andrew Hammond. A selection of old and new carols include the Sussex Carol (arrangement by Vaughan Williams), There Is No Rose (Elizabeth Maconchy) and O Holy Night (Adolphe Adam, arrangement by JE West). The Christmas story is told in the words of the King James Bible, with poems by Patrick Carey and Ruth Padel(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace and Cherry Healey find out how favourite festive party foods are made. Gregg visits an enormous factory in Nottingham where 200,000 canapes are produced every 24 hours. Cherry discovers fail-safe scientific methods for cooking the perfect Christmas roast, turning her oven into a turkey sauna by popping ice cubes into a baking tray. Historian Ruth Goodman looks into the tradition of kissing under the mistletoe and also investigates how the period of advent, traditionally associated with fasting, came to be connected with chocolate-packed calendars(n)

programme.title: The Planets
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox tells the life story of the solar system, beginning with the development of the four rocky worlds closest to the Sun - Mercury, Venus, Earth and Mars. They were born at the same time from the same material - yet have lived radically different lives. What immense forces are at play?(n)

programme.title: Dancing the Nutcracker - Inside the Royal Ballet
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes with the ballet company, following the three months leading up to the opening of this year's Christmas production of Tchaikovsky's ballet. The programme follows a number of dancers who are making their first performances in iconic roles, including Francesca Hayward as the Sugar Plum Fairy, as well as revealing the work of the highly skilled backstage team(n)

programme.title: Gareth Malone's Christmas Concert
programme.desc: The choirmaster puts on a show for the hard-pressed staff at Watford General Hospital, who are among the 400,000 NHS employees who will be giving up their Christmas Day this year to care for patients. Responding to a message from the chief executive of the hard-working community hospital who wants to give her workers a much-needed festive boost, Gareth sets to work enlisting the help of staff, patients and family members to produce a spectacular concert full of wonder for everyone to enjoy(n)

programme.title: Scrooge (1970)
programme.desc: Hard-hearted Victorian miser Ebenezer Scrooge refuses to join in with the spirit of the festive season - until a succession of spectral visitors turn up to teach him the error of his ways. Musical adaptation of Charles Dickens' classic A Christmas Carol, starring Albert Finney, Alec Guinness, Kenneth More, Edith Evans and Paddy Stone. The film is preceded by an introductory interview with Albert Finney from the set in 1970(n)

programme.title: The Queen
programme.desc: Her Majesty Queen Elizabeth II delivers her annual address in the Christmas Message to the Nation and Commonwealth(n)

programme.title: Raymond Briggs: Snowmen, Bogeymen & Milkmen
programme.desc: Profile of the author and illustrator, marking the 40th anniversary of the original publication of The Snowman. The programme explores Briggs' other work, including Fungus the Bogeyman, Father Christmas, When the Wind Blows and Ethel & Ernest, and includes animated interludes with artwork by Chris Riddell. Interviews with Briggs and contributions from Andy Serkis, Nick Park, Steve Bell and Posy Simmonds feature throughout. Narrated by Brenda Blethyn(n)

programme.title: Upstart Crow
programme.desc: Will is busily rehearsing his latest play, which is to be performed as part of the queen's Christmas festivities - but which may not be quite ready for a royal audience. Back in Stratford, the Shakespeares are planning to enjoy a quiet Christmas, but find themselves inundated with uninvited guests - including Will's arch enemy Robert Greene, who claims to have changed his wicked ways. Emma Thompson guest stars as Elizabeth I(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Frazer is unable to hide his delight when Captain Mainwaring's disreputable brother gatecrashes a sherry party for local dignitaries and tries to reclaim a pocket watch his father promised him. Comedy first aired in 1975, starring Arthur Lowe in a dual role, with John Le Mesurier and John Laurie(n)

programme.title: The Morecambe and Wise Christmas Show
programme.desc: Memorable moments from a decade of the comedy duo's Christmas shows, featuring appearances by newsreader Angela Rippon, singer Shirley Bassey and conductor Andre Previn, who all show a different side to their personalities(n)

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman hosts the third round of the festive quiz featuring teams of famous graduates. This episode sees Actors Monica Dolan and Bob Barrett, representing Guildhall School of Music and Drama, in a festive contest with presenter Maryam Moshiri and Arctic explorer Pen Hadow, both former students at UCL(n)

programme.title: Dolly Parton - Here I Am
programme.desc: An intimate portrait of the acclaimed songwriter, which invites viewers to get to know the real Dolly Parton, the woman behind the global phenomenon. The feature-length documentary, directed by Francis Whately, takes the audience on a musical journey through some of her greatest songs, including The Bridge, I Will Always Love You, and 9 To 5. Cameras also capture Dolly celebrating the 50th anniversary of her very first performance at the Grand Ole Opry, and features unprecedented access to the singer herself, as well as collaborators from throughout her career(n)

programme.title: Glastonbury 2014
programme.desc: The complete set by the 'queen of country music' in front of one of the biggest crowds the festival's Pyramid stage had ever seen. Featuring self-penned classics such as Jolene, Coat of Many Colours, 9 to 5, Islands in the Stream and I Will Always Love You(n)

programme.title: Glastonbury 2019
programme.desc: Lauren Laverne and Clara Amfo introduce Kylie Minogue's performance on the Pyramid Stage. This set marked the singer's first appearance at the festival, after being forced to pull out for health reasons in 2005, and featured hits from throughout her 30-year career. Plus, highlights from Miley Cyrus and Koffee's performances, and a film introduced by the Rev Kate Bottley exploring the spiritual history of Glastonbury(n)

programme.title: Merry Christmas Baby - With Gregory Porter & Friends
programme.desc: The singer presents a seasonal mix of warm and joyful music with a jazz vibe. Special guests including songstress Corinne Bailey Rae and saxophonist Courtney Pine join Gregory for an evening of traditional Christmas classics alongside jazz standards, filmed in front of an audience in Cardiff Bay. The show has all the ingredients of a perfect winter warmer, making it ideal viewing on a mellow Christmas night. Performances include Merry Christmas Baby, Silent Night, Take Me to the Alley and The Christmas Song(n)

programme.title: Moana (2016)
programme.desc: To save her island home from a terrible curse, a brave Polynesian teenager must set sail and journey into the great blue yonder with the aid of a legendary hero. Disney animation, with the voices of Dwayne Johnson, Auli'i Cravalho, Rachel House and Temuera Morrison(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace and Cherry Healey find out how favourite festive party foods are made. Gregg visits an enormous factory in Nottingham where 200,000 canapes are produced every 24 hours. Cherry discovers fail-safe scientific methods for cooking the perfect Christmas roast, turning her oven into a turkey sauna by popping ice cubes into a baking tray. Historian Ruth Goodman looks into the tradition of kissing under the mistletoe and also investigates how the period of advent, traditionally associated with fasting, came to be connected with chocolate-packed calendars(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Documentary following life on farms around Scotland, from Argyll to the Outer Hebrides. This time, a new family of dairy farmers, the McLeans, are introduced. Meanwhile, Raymond goes the extra mile to save his lambs, Stevie faces buffalo cow number 666, and Kirsty and Aimee take last year's calves to market(n)

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: Five pairs of travellers - Sue and Clare, Felix and Josh, Natalie and Shameema, Jinda and Bindu, and Darron and Alex - set off from Greenwich, south-east London, passing through five checkpoints to reach their final destination of Singapore - without getting on a plane or using mobile phones or credit cards. Their first port of call is the ancient Greek city of Delphi. The stakes are high - everyone wants to be first to the finish line to win the cash prize(n)

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The five teams head to their next checkpoint - Baku in Azerbaijan - a journey that will see them leave behind the familiarity of the West for the exotic and unknown of the East. Their need for speed and travelling smart is more pressing than ever, as the team arriving last in Baku will be eliminated from the competition. Fed up with the strops thrown at any slight hurdle by his unemployed son Alex, Darron decides that a culture shock could be just the thing to sort him out - but will it be at the cost of their place in the competition?(n)

programme.title: The Planets
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox continues his tour of the solar system revealing that it was once home to not one, but two blue planets. Today, Mars is an arid desert world that long ago had the same potential as Earth. Which begs a big question - did life get a start on both worlds?(n)

programme.title: Oklahoma! (1955)
programme.desc: A farm girl is torn between two very different suitors - a charming cowboy wandering through the territory and a brooding farmhand with a dark side to his nature. Meanwhile, another cattle driver has finally raised enough money to marry his sweetheart, only to find he has a rival. Rodgers and Hammerstein's musical Western, with Gordon MacRae, Shirley Jones and Rod Steiger. Songs include Oh What a Beautiful Mornin' and I Cain't Say No(n)

programme.title: Guys and Dolls (1955)
programme.desc: A gambler accepts a bet that he cannot win the heart of a strait-laced Salvation Army member. Meanwhile, his friend has to find a secret location for an illegal dice game he is organising - while also contending with the police, a mobster and a girlfriend desperate to drag him to the altar. Musical, starring Frank Sinatra, Marlon Brando and Jean Simmons. Songs include Luck Be a Lady Tonight and Sit Down You're Rocking the Boat(n)

programme.title: The Marvellous World of Roald Dahl
programme.desc: Documentary making use of archived letters, writings and biographical material to tell the story of the life of Roald Dahl, in his own inimitable voice. From his time as a fighter pilot in the Second World War, through to his work as a spy and his encounters with Walt Disney and Theodore Roosevelt, the programme explores how Dahl's personal experiences may have informed the fantastical tales he would eventually publish, including Charlie and the Chocolate Factory and Matilda. Includes contributions by Dahl's last wife Liccy, his daughter Lucy and biographer Donald Sturrock. Long-term collaborator Quentin Blake also provides new animated illustrations based on the events of Dahl's life(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Will Perry presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Porridge (1979)
programme.desc: Cellmates Fletcher and Godber are coerced into aiding an escape attempt organised by the sinister Grouty. However, the two jailbirds contrive to break back into prison before their absence is detected by the formidable Mr Mackay. Comedy based on the TV series, starring Ronnie Barker, Richard Beckinsale, Peter Vaughan, Fulton Mackay and Brian Wilde(n)

programme.title: The Many Faces of
programme.desc: The story of a quiet, dedicated man who might have been a bank manager, but went on to become one of the nation's most popular comedy stars. Ben Elton, Michael Grade, David Renwick and Josephine Tewson are among friends and colleagues who remember his partnership with Ronnie Corbett in sketch show The Two Ronnies, as skinflint grocer Arkwright in Open All Hours and crafty prisoner Fletch in Porridge. There is also a look at his prolific writing career and his work as an actor in serious roles(n)

programme.title: The Two Ronnies: The Studio Recordings
programme.desc: A collection of unedited takes from the classic comedy show, featuring both famous and more obscure sketches from programmes recorded in 1979 and 1980. There is also footage of Ronnie Barker and Ronnie Corbett entertaining the studio audience during breaks in filming one of their celebrated newsdesk sketches(n)

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman hosts as a team from the University of Liverpool, including actress Anna Maxwell Martin and broadcaster Roger Bolton, faces a team from Hull, with comedian Lucy Beaumont and novelist Tracy Borman(n)

programme.title: Paddington: The Man Behind the Bear
programme.desc: Following straight on from the premiere of Paddington 2 over on BBC One, this documentary explores where the original stories came from. Creator Michael Bond drew on his wartime memories of evacuee children to shape the story of the little bear from darkest Peru, while his literary agent, who came from a Jewish family who fled the Nazis, inspired Paddington's kindly friend Mr Gruber. Featuring interviews with Michael Bond's family, friends and celebrity admirers, including Stephen Fry, Bernard Cribbins and Heston Blumenthal, and readings by Hugh Bonneville(n)

programme.title: A Life on Screen
programme.desc: From his early days in comedy troupe The Jockeys of Norfolk, to his breakthrough role as Charles in Four Weddings and a Funeral, Hugh Grant has become one of Britain's best-loved stars, with hits including two Bridget Jones movies, Notting Hill, Love Actually, and Paddington 2. More recently, he has turned his talents to television in the acclaimed drama A Very English Scandal, playing notorious politician Jeremy Thorpe. Aside from the Bafta-winning actor discussing his career, friends and colleagues such as Andie MacDowell, Richard Curtis, Sandra Bullock, Colin Firth and Nicholas Hoult also add their thoughts(n)

programme.title: Florence Foster Jenkins (2016)
programme.desc: A tone deaf socialite imagines she is a great singer, and her conniving yet devoted husband allows her to remain in her fantasy by holding concerts with small, invited audiences. However, reality threatens to intrude when she announces her intention of realising a lifelong dream of performing in New York's Carnegie Hall, recruiting a struggling young pianist to aid her. Fact-based comedy, starring Meryl Streep and Hugh Grant(n)

programme.title: James May: The Reassembler
programme.desc: In a festive special, James puts together a Hornby Flying Scotsman, his favourite childhood Christmas present, which he was given in 1972 and sent him on to a path of mechanical intrigue and reassembly. Laid out in all its 138 tiny component parts, James lovingly reassembles the train as well as his memories of Christmases and toys past(n)

programme.title: The Last Igloo
programme.desc: Documentary following a day in the life of an Inuit hunter in Greenland, who builds one of the traditional ice dwellings while fishing through the sea ice. While igloos are no longer used as permanent homes, the traditional skills behind their construction are still used in building temporary shelters in the wilderness. The film captures this ingenious craft, which may be lost to the world as the ice sheet disappears(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A collection of chart-toppers from festive episodes of the music show, including the first-ever repeat showing of a recently rediscovered 1967 performance by the Rolling Stones. The programme also features perennial Christmas classics from the likes of Slade, Mud and Frankie Goes to Hollywood, as well as Ian Dury & the Blockheads, Madness, East 17, Coldplay, the Human League and the Pet Shop Boys(n)

programme.title: Martin's Close
programme.desc: In 1684, John Martin is on trial for his life, but this is not a cut and dried murder case because the young woman he is accused of killing has been seen after her death. Mark Gatiss's adaptation of MR James' ghost story, starring Peter Capaldi, Wilf Scolding, Simon Williams, Sara Crowe, James Holmes and Fisayo Akinade(n)

programme.title: The Turn of the Screw
programme.desc: A governess is sent to take care of two orphans living in a remote country house, where she is unsettled by the presence of a pair of ghosts who seem to come and go as they please - and soon suspects the youngsters know more about the spectres than they are letting on. Her anxiety builds when she learns that the previous governess, who was very close to the children, died with her lover in mysterious circumstances. Chilling one-off drama, based on Henry James' classic 1898 novella and updated to a Twenties setting. Michelle Dockery, Sue Johnston, Mark Umbers, Josef Lindsay and Eva Sayer star(n)

programme.title: The Lost Ghost Story with Mark Gatiss
programme.desc: The actor and writer examines the life and career of author MR James, trying to discover how this devout Anglican bachelor created supernatural tales that continue to chill readers more than a century on. He visits James's childhood home in Suffolk as well as Eton College and King's College, Cambridge, the two institutions where he spent most of his life, and ventures into ancient churches, dark cloisters and echoing libraries along the way as he attempt to uncover the writer's inspirations(n)

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Mark Radcliffe delves into the BBC vaults to present a selection of Christmas music, featuring perennial classics from the likes of John & Yoko, Slade, Wizzard, Wham!, Jona Lewie, the Pogues featuring Kirsty MacColl and Paul McCartney. Plus, there is a new festive performance by Boyzone, a peek at the Killers' annual Christmas video and archive performances by Teardrop Explodes, Darts, Mud, Emeli Sande, the Hollies and Jimmy Osmond(n)

programme.title: Nothing Like a Dame
programme.desc: Eileen Atkins, Judi Dench, Joan Plowright and Maggie Smith let the cameras in on a friendship that goes back more than half a century. Coming together for a weekend at the country retreat Dame Joan once shared with her husband Laurence Olivier, the acting greats discuss their lives and careers, reminisce about their humble beginnings in the theatre, and reflect on love and loss(n)

programme.title: Magic Flute from Glyndebourne
programme.desc: Mozart's The Magic Flute with the Orchestra of the Age of Enlightenment, conducted by Ryan Wigglesworth. From Glyndebourne Festival 2019. With David Portillo as Tamino, Björn Bürger as Papageno, Sofia Fomina as Pamina, Caroline Wettergreen as Queen of the Night and Brindley Sherratt as Sarastro(n)

programme.title: Detectorists
programme.desc: Lance has failed to detect even so much as a ring-pull in months, leaving Russell to suspect he has been cursed. Seasonal episode of the comedy, starring Mackenzie Crook, Toby Jones and Pearce Quigley(n)

programme.title: The Duchess (2008)
programme.desc: Fact-based period drama, chronicling the life of Georgiana Spencer who became Duchess of Devonshire in 1774 at the age of 17. Adored by the public for her extravagant lifestyle, Spencer endured a troubled marriage and was eventually driven into the arms of Charles Grey, a senior Whig politician. Starring Keira Knightley, Ralph Fiennes, Dominic Cooper and Charlotte Rampling(n)

programme.title: Inspector Montalbano
programme.desc: The curmudgeonly Sicilian detective investigates the killing of a local businessman. The victim's widow alleges his Tunisian lover, who has mysteriously disappeared, was responsible, and Montalbano begins to believe the case is linked to another suspicious death that occurred that day. As the inspector searches for the suspect, he manages to find her five-year-old son, and asks his partner Livia to look after him - little suspecting how attached she will grow to the boy. Italian detective drama, based on the novels by Andrea Camilleri, starring Luca Zingaretti. In Italian(n)

programme.title: Tom Jones & Beverley Knight's Gospel Christmas
programme.desc: Tom Jones and Beverley Knight host a festive concert from Cardiff featuring a blend of traditional gospel, carols and songs of spiritual intent by modern greats, such as Prince and Bob Dylan, performed by choirs and a house band from the British gospel scene(n)

programme.title: What We Were Watching
programme.desc: Grace Dent embarks on a televisual trip back in time as she immerses herself in the sights, sounds and schedules of TV from 40 years ago. As well as exploring what the nation's viewers enjoyed watching on the box , Grace sees if she can beat that year's Mastermind grand finalist, and sees how well she fares on The Generation Game's famous conveyor belt game(n)

programme.title: The Sound of Movie Musicals with Neil Brand
programme.desc: The composer and musician fronts a three-part series on the movie musical, from Hollywood to China and the USSR, and from Snow White and Gene Kelly to Grease and La La Land. In this opening episode, Brand argues that the movie musical was the most important form of cinema from the advent of the age of sound. Beginning with the very first film musical, 1929's Broadway Melody, he looks at the huge and lasting impact of the genre and, in his trademark analysis of songs at the piano, analyses some of the most important numbers in this first 'Golden Age'(n)

programme.title: Royal Institution Christmas Lectures 2019
programme.desc: Mathematician Hannah Fry presents three talks examining the hidden numbers, rules and patterns that govern everyday life, beginning with the secrets of luck and what really controls destiny. She explores whether people really can predict the future - from the chances of snow at Christmas to dodging erupting volcanoes. She also meets the maths gurus behind Liverpool football club's winning streak, to spill the beans on how analysing numbers can give the team an edge in the Premier League, and reveals the tricks to perfecting a Christmas cracker pull to win the prize every time(n)

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: The Meldrews embark on a two-week holiday in Portugal in David Renwick's feature-length special from 1993. However, there are one or two disasters Victor does not quite believe, such as rising damp, a plague of mice and a troublesome boxer - not forgetting the dreaded donkeys. Guest starring Joan Sims and Peter Cook(n)

programme.title: Comedy Connections
programme.desc: The story of David Renwick's classic sitcom One Foot in the Grave, which introduced the world to grumpy suburbanite Victor Meldrew (Richard Wilson), whose irritation at modern life reflected that of his creator. In addition to Wilson himself, those taking part include Annette Crosbie, who played Victor's long-suffering wife(n)

programme.title: British Sitcom: 60 Years of Laughing at Ourselves
programme.desc: A journey through the history of the British TV sitcom, from Hancock's Half Hour in the 1950s to present day shows, revealing the inspiration behind some of the most enduring characters and how comedy has reflected changing attitudes in society. Narrated by Rebecca Front, and featuring contributions from writers and actors including Steve Coogan, Richard Curtis, James Corden, Graham Linehan, Dick Clement and Ian La Frenais(n)

programme.title: Secret Life of Boys
programme.desc: Ginger plans on getting her cousins to Australia by any means necessary. She decides to prank call them until they give up and get on that plane. Ginger's plan doesn't go well though and both Corey and Chris are unphased by her ‘Ginger Snaps'. Matt tries to get Robbie to admit that he's the reason the trip is cancelled, but Matt inadvertently tells Robbie his own secret: Matt is spending Christmas with Tabitha's family.
