programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life(n)

programme.title: Upstairs Downstairs
programme.desc: It is January 1936, and diplomat Sir Hallam and Lady Agnes Holland (Ed Stoppard and Keeley Hawes) move into 165 Eaton Place - closely followed by his widowed mother and her wayward sister. But they also need a full serving staff, for which Lady Agnes turns to an agency run by none other than Rose Buck (Jean Marsh), who used to work at the house. Eileen Atkins, Art Malik, Anne Reid and Adrian Scarborough co-star(n)

programme.title: Battle of Britain: The Real Story
programme.desc: James Holland explores the Battle of Britain from the German point of view. The author focuses on the tactics, technology and intelligence available to both nations, and examines how they decided to use their resources. He also gains access to first-hand testimonies from German pilots, and diaries and documents that reveal a previously unknown side to this confrontation(n)

programme.title: The Queen's Palaces
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce explores the stories behind official royal residences, which act as family homes and places to conduct the business of monarchy, and charts the evolution of their architecture. She begins with Buckingham Palace and learns about its links to London sewers, its features including state rooms and a home for camels and elephants, plus how its chandeliers are cleaned(n)

programme.title: Anoop and The Elephant (1972)
programme.desc: Two English children meet an Indian boy and his pet elephant, and help to keep the animal out of the hands of a greedy circus owner. Family drama, with Anoop Singh and Phil Daniels(n)

programme.title: For the Love of Ada (1972)
programme.desc: A pensioner goes to great lengths to celebrate her wedding anniversary. Comedy, based on the 1970s sitcom, starring Irene Handl and Wilfred Pickles(n)

programme.title: The Case of the Frightened Lady (1940)
programme.desc: A woman employed as a secretary to her aristocratic aunt has to contend with sinister servants plotting behind her back and a scheme by her employer to marry her off to her own cousin. The situation is complicated further when the family's doctor is accused of murder. Thriller, starring Marius Goring and Penelope Dudley-Ward(n)

programme.title: Upstairs Downstairs
programme.desc: It is five months since the Hollands moved into Eaton Place, and Rose is officially installed as housekeeper, while Lady Agnes has manoeuvred the house to the centre of London society - and her joy is compounded by the news she is pregnant. But storm clouds are gathering in Europe, and Lady Persie falls under the spell of Oswald Mosley and his British Union of Fascists, forming an alliance that will drag her into moral and physical peril. Keeley Hawes, Jean Marsh and Claire Foy star(n)

programme.title: London Live's Panto Review
programme.desc: From Cinderella to Pinocchio, Alicia Edwards meets the stars of London's Christmas Pantomimes(n)

programme.title: The Queen's Palaces
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce explores the history of Windsor Castle, which has evolved over more than 1,000 years from its original role as a fortress and was nearly destroyed by a fire in 1992. Inside the residence is a musket ball that killed a naval hero, jewel-encrusted gold and silver table decorations, and Queen Mary's doll's house - complete with running water and a secret garden(n)

programme.title: Golden Years (2016)
programme.desc: A retired couple face a grim future after a financial crisis costs them both their pensions. Refusing to take the situation lying down, they resolve to take back what is rightfully theirs with a series of daring bank robberies. Comedy, starring Bernard Hill, Virginia McKenna, Simon Callow and Sue Johnston(n)

programme.title: Chubby Funny (2016)
programme.desc: An aspiring actor continually blames others for his failure to become a big star. He decides to give himself a year living in London to finally land his big break - but the personal crises that have dogged him all his life are not going away. Comedy drama, directed by and starring Harry Michell, with Augustus Prew and Jeff Rawle(n)

programme.title: Reservoir Dogs (1992)
programme.desc: Criminals are employed by a crime boss to carry out a diamond robbery that goes disastrously wrong. In the bloody aftermath of the heist, they try to establish what happened and how the police were aware of their plans all along - leading to the hardening suspicion that one of them betrayed the gang. Quentin Tarantino's thriller, starring Harvey Keitel, Tim Roth, Michael Madsen, Chris Penn and Steve Buscemi(n)

programme.title: Confessions of a Sex Maniac (1974)
programme.desc: An architect comes up with the idea of designing a building shaped like a woman's breast. He finds himself short of inspiration, and goes in search of a woman whose figure exactly matches what he wants his project to look like. Comedy, starring Roger Lloyd Pack and Vicki Hodge(n)

programme.title: Food Junkies
programme.desc: Joey Bones selects his favourite moments from the series that cuts straight to the core of London's food scene(n)

programme.title: Upstairs Downstairs
programme.desc: It is five months since the Hollands moved into Eaton Place, and Rose is officially installed as housekeeper, while Lady Agnes has manoeuvred the house to the centre of London society - and her joy is compounded by the news she is pregnant. programme.title: Codebreaker
programme.desc: The first of a two-part docu-drama uncovering the secret of how a 24-year-old maths student and a GPO engineer hacked into Hitler's personal code machine - not the Enigma coding system, but something even more intimidating known as his 'secret writer'. Despite his achievements, codebreaker Alan Turing's life was dogged by tragedy(n)

programme.title: The Queen's Palaces
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce explores the history of Windsor Castle, which has evolved over more than 1,000 years from its original role as a fortress and was nearly destroyed by a fire in 1992. Inside the residence is a musket ball that killed a naval hero, jewel-encrusted gold and silver table decorations, and Queen Mary's doll's house - complete with running water and a secret garden(n)

programme.title: Pop Pirates (1984)
programme.desc: A pop group enters a talent contest, but their chances are jeopardised by the lead singer's involvement in video piracy. Drama, starring Spencer Chandler and Roger Daltrey(n)

programme.title: Where Angels Fear to Tread (1991)
programme.desc: A wealthy widow travels to Italy to escape the pressure of her in-laws, and promptly falls in love with a poor local. When the revived woman agrees to marry her foreign friend, her relatives back home are shocked by the apparent disregard for class, culture and their family's image, and set about putting an end to the unlikely union. Period drama, based on the novel by EM Forster, starring Helena Bonham Carter, Judy Davis and Rupert Graves(n)

programme.title: Operation Cupid (1960)
programme.desc: A Cockney wide boy is running a struggling matrimonial agency. When a wealthy widow turns up looking for a husband, he sees the chance to become rich by posing as a South African millionaire and romancing her himself. Comedy, starring Charles Farrell, Avice Landone and Wallas Eaton(n)

programme.title: Upstairs Downstairs
programme.desc: Lady Persie's life has become increasingly complex, as she continues her illicit affair and pursues her right-wing political interests with little regard for the potential danger to those she loves. Meanwhile, Lady Agnes prepares for motherhood by having her picture taken by celebrated photographer Cecil Beaton - a visit that causes excitement for one particular member of the downstairs staff - and Sir Hallam finds himself dragged into the crisis surrounding the abdication of Edward VIII facing the British monarchy. Keeley Hawes, Ed Stoppard, Anne Reid and Claire Foy star in the period drama(n)

programme.title: Our Queen
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary charting an extraordinary year in the life of Her Majesty, Queen Elizabeth II(n)

programme.title: The Queen's Palaces
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce looks into the past of Edinburgh's Palace of Holyroodhouse, which is located close to the dormant volcano Arthur's Seat. Events in the royal residence, one of the smallest palaces in Britain, have determined the futures of England, France and Scotland, and it was the last place in which a royal prince challenged the right of an English king to sit on the throne. Fiona also examines objects including the Darnley jewel, which features numerous hidden messages, and the needlework of a doomed queen(n)

programme.title: A Random Encounter (2013)
programme.desc: Two strangers trying to make their way in Hollywood meet by chance in a coffee shop, and come away unable to stop thinking about one another. However, they both soon discover that they have no way of tracking the other down. Romantic comedy, starring Abby Wathen, Michael Rady, Meghan Markle and Sean Young(n)

programme.title: Dying of the Light (2014)
programme.desc: A CIA agent is diagnosed with a degenerative condition that results in him being forced to retire from the world of intelligence. When he receives word that a terrorist who once tortured him has resurfaced, he goes rogue and sets out to assassinate the man while he is still able. Thriller, starring Nicolas Cage, Anton Yelchin and Irene Jacob(n)

programme.title: One Crazy Thing (2016)
programme.desc: An actor's career collapses after a series of mistakes, and ends up an online laughing stock when his ex-girlfriend leaks their sex tape. He finds new hope in a relationship with a woman who has turned her back on the internet - leaving him wondering how or even if to tell her about the embarrassing footage. Romantic comedy, starring Ray Panthaki and Daisy Bevan(n)

programme.title: Escort Girls (1974)
programme.desc: Seven people who are alone in London on Christmas eve turn to an escort agency for comfort. However, things do not work out as simply as planned, as they wrestle with their personal demons and endure traumatic experiences of violence, racism and crime. Drama, starring Maria O'Brien and David Dixon(n)

programme.desc: The Dumpling Sisters share their favourite moments from the series(n)

programme.title: I Was There: The Great War Interviews
programme.desc: In the early 1960s, the BBC spoke to almost 300 people about their experiences during the First World War for landmark series The Great War, but only a fraction of the interviews were broadcast. This programme airs a selection of the original material, most of which has never been screened before, with recollections of the horrors of artillery bombardment and fleeting outbreaks of peace on the battlefield, as well as the stories of those on the home front waiting for news of their loved ones. Narrated by Samuel West(n)

programme.title: The Queen's Palaces
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce looks into the past of Edinburgh's Palace of Holyroodhouse, which is located close to the dormant volcano Arthur's Seat. Events in the royal residence, one of the smallest palaces in Britain, have determined the futures of England, France and Scotland, and it was the last place in which a royal prince challenged the right of an English king to sit on the throne. Fiona also examines objects including the Darnley jewel, which features numerous hidden messages, and the needlework of a doomed queen(n)

programme.title: Cup Fever (1965)
programme.desc: A crooked councillor tries to make life difficult for a children's football team, so they will lose a match against his son's side. However, help is at hand from Manchester United manager Matt Busby. Children's drama, starring David Lodge and Busby as himself, with cameos from a host of football stars(n)

programme.title: Operation Cupid (1960)
programme.desc: A Cockney wide boy is running a struggling matrimonial agency. programme.title: The Golden Salamander (1950)
programme.desc: An archaeologist gets involved in gun-running while travelling to North Africa to collect some valuable antiques. While reluctant to cause trouble at first, a murder convinces him he must help bring the criminals to justice. Thriller, starring Trevor Howard and Herbert Lom(n)

programme.title: Upstairs Downstairs
programme.desc: Drama and intrigue with the aristocrats and serving staff of 165 Eaton Place as it opens its doors once more. September 1938, and as dark clouds gather over Europe, Hallam's preoccupation with Nazi Germany leads him into dangerous waters overseas. Back home, Lady Agnes is frail following the birth of her second child, and finds great support in the forthright Aunt Blanche, who has made herself comfortable at Eaton Place following the funeral of her sister Lady Holland. In the servants' quarters, Rose is taken ill with TB, so the staff welcome spirited new maid Beryl, who catches Harry's eye, while events upstairs end in a startling revelation about Mr Pritchard. Keeley Hawes, Ed Stoppard, Alex Kingston and Adrian Scarborough star(n)

programme.title: Our Queen

programme.title: Prince Harry: Frontline Afghanistan
programme.desc: Documentary following Prince Harry on his Army tour of Afghanistan, which came to an end in January. The film offers an insight into his deployment as an Apache attack helicopter gunner, and the 28-year-old, who was on his second military visit to the country, also talks about his experiences. Presented by Richard Bacon(n)

programme.title: The Face of Love (2013)
programme.desc: A widow begins a new relationship with a man who is the exact double of her husband, and shares many of his personality traits. Over time, she grows to love his own unique qualities, but fears he will leave her if he ever discovers the resemblance. Romantic drama, starring Annette Bening, Ed Harris and Robin Williams(n)

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner(n)

programme.title: Winter Wipeout
programme.desc: Richard Hammond presents this chilly version of Total Wipeout, which sees contestants including a former world-class athlete and a binman wrapping up warm to tackle a snow-covered course with winter-themed obstacles and seasonal challenges. They must manoeuvre themselves past Granny's House, watching out for the angry dog nipping at their ankles and a front door guaranteed to smack them in the face. They then cling onto the ski lift to avoid plunging into the icy waters, before riding the conveyor belt that throws them onto those old Wipeout favourites, the big red balls. Amanda Byram laughs it up at the side of the course(n)
